get.description:
Liveness probe. No I/O.

Returns ``200 OK`` with the build version. The point is to give
container runtimes something cheap and reliable to poll: a
process that can answer this endpoint is process-alive.

schema.title: Response Health Api V1 Health Get

get.description:
Readiness probe. Issues ``SELECT 1`` with a hard timeout.

Returns ``200 OK`` if the database is reachable, ``503`` if it
is not. Never raises - the response object is mutated in place.

schema.title: Response Health Ready Api V1 Health Ready Get

get.description:
System information for the dashboard settings page.

Requires authentication - exposes database version, Python
version, and package details that should not be public.

schema.title: Response Health System Api V1 Health System Get

post.description:
Authenticate a user and mint a session.

On any failure (wrong email, wrong password, locked, inactive)
raises :class:`AuthenticationError` which the error middleware
maps to 401 with the byte-identical envelope. The caller cannot
distinguish between the failure modes.

get.description:
Return the current user with their project memberships.

The dashboard's project switcher reads this on every page
load to know which projects to render in the sidebar and what
role the user holds in each.

patch.description:
Update the current user's display name and/or timezone.

Only fields present in the request body are changed. Returns
the full ``UserMePublic`` payload (same shape as ``GET /me``)
so the dashboard can update its local state in one round-trip.

post.description:
Self-service password change for the current user.

Verifies the current password, validates the new password
against the policy, hashes it, and rotates the session atomic-
ally:

1. ``update_password_hash`` writes the new hash and bumps
   ``password_changed_at``.
2. ``sessions.revoke_all_for_user`` explicitly revokes every
   prior session (audit H3 - the timestamp check alone leaves
   stale ``revoked_at IS NULL`` rows that still show in the
   Account â†’ Security tab until each is next touched).
3. A fresh session is minted + cookies reset so the caller
   keeps working without an extra login round-trip.

get.description:
Return every active (non-revoked, non-expired) session for the
current user.

The ``is_current`` flag marks which session corresponds to the
cookie that made this request, so the dashboard can highlight
"this device" in the sessions list.

schema.title: Response List Sessions Api V1 Auth Sessions Get

post.description:
Revoke a specific session belonging to the current user.

The session must exist and belong to the authenticated user -
users cannot revoke other users' sessions. Returns 404 if the
session does not exist or does not belong to the caller.

get.description:
Return whether the brain is in first-boot mode.

Public - no auth required. The dashboard chrome calls this on
every page load to decide whether to show the setup CTA.

post.description: Verify the setup token and bootstrap the brain.

get.description:
Serve the inline HTML form.

Only available while ``users`` is empty. After the bootstrap
admin exists, returns 404 - the form has no business existing
after first boot. The token query parameter is intentionally
NOT used by the server; the JS reads it from
``window.location.search`` and stuffs it into the hidden form
field. The server's only job is to gate visibility on the
first-boot flag.

List the projects the current user has access to.

When the caller is authenticated via a **project-scoped Bearer
key**, the response is further filtered to only that bound
project â€” the scope layer writes
``request.state.api_key_project_slug`` at auth time and this
handler respects it. Without this filter a project-A-bound
key could enumerate every project the owner user can see
(external audit, Critical #1).

schema.title: Response List Projects Api V1 Projects Get

delete.description:
Soft-archive a project (sets ``is_active=False``).

We never hard-delete projects - that would cascade across the
audit log and break the historical record. The archived
project is hidden from list views but its rows survive.

schema.title: Response List Agents Api V1 Projects  Slug  Agents Get

post.description:
Mint a new agent token.

The plaintext token is returned ONCE - only the HMAC hash is
stored. Operators must paste the plaintext into their agent
configuration; if they lose it they have to mint another.

get.description:
Catalogue of scopes the UI can offer at mint time.

Unauthenticated by design - the shape is static and helps
docs/tooling without leaking anything sensitive. Deployments
that want to hide this can strip the endpoint at a reverse
proxy; the brain does not.

List all active (non-revoked) API keys for the current user.

Never returns the plaintext token - only the prefix for
identification.

schema.title: Response List Api Keys Api V1 Api Keys Get

post.description:
Create a new personal API key.

The plaintext token is returned ONCE in the response. Only the
HMAC-SHA256 hash is stored. If the user loses the plaintext they
must revoke the key and create a new one.

Token format: ``z4k_`` prefix + 32 bytes urlsafe base64.

delete.description:
Revoke (soft-delete) a personal API key.

Sets ``revoked_at`` on the key. The key can no longer be used
for authentication but remains in the database for audit
purposes.

get.description:
List tasks with filtering, search, pagination, and export.

New filters (Phase A):
- ``priority`` - comma-separated: ``?priority=critical,high``
- ``search`` - full-text search across name, queue, worker
- ``worker`` - exact match on worker_name
- ``until`` - upper bound on received_at (pair with ``since``)
- ``format`` - ``csv`` or ``xlsx`` for export (overrides pagination)

Return the full canvas (chain / group / chord) tree this task belongs to.

Looks up the requested task, walks to its root via
``root_task_id`` (or treats the task as its own root if the
field is null), then returns every task in the project sharing
that root. Capped at 500 nodes so a runaway chain doesn't
return an unbounded blob; the response carries ``truncated:
true`` when the cap kicks in so the dashboard can show a
"showing first 500 of N" notice.

post.description:
Delete task records from the brain database.

Supports two modes:
- By ID list: provide ``task_ids`` (up to 1000).
- By filter: provide ``filter_*`` params (deletes up to 10000).

This is a brain-side operation (no agent command needed).
Requires ADMIN role.

schema.title: Response List Workers Api V1 Projects  Slug  Workers Get

get.description:
Get detailed worker info including inspect() data.

Uses the worker's UUID (not hostname) so URLs don't break on
special characters like ``@``.

schema.title: Response List Queues Api V1 Projects  Slug  Queues Get

post.description:
DESTRUCTIVE - requires admin role.

Removes every pending task from the named queue. The agent's
purge action refuses the destructive ``queue_delete`` fallback
(B3 audit fix), so this is bounded to ``queue_purge`` semantics.
Grow or shrink the worker pool by ``delta`` processes.
Start consuming from an additional queue on a worker.
Stop consuming from a queue on a worker.

Set or clear a per-task rate limit on one (or every) worker.

The target_id on the audit row is the task name, not the worker
name - the rate limit is a property of the task across the
cluster, not of the worker. Operators searching the audit log
for a noisy task want to find every rate-limit change against
that task in one query.

schema.title: Response List Schedules Api V1 Projects  Slug  Schedules Get

post.description:
Issue a one-shot ``schedule.trigger_now`` command.

The schedule itself is unchanged - its normal cadence is
untouched. The agent fires the underlying task once.

get.description:
List audit log entries for one project.

Requires admin role on the project - audit reads are
privileged because they can reveal who did what when, which
is itself sensitive.

parameters.name: format

schema.description: Optional export format. When set, pagination is ignored and the full filter result (capped at 50 000 rows) is returned as a file download.
schema.title: Format

parameters.description: Optional export format. When set, pagination is ignored and the full filter result (capped at 50 000 rows) is returned as a file download.

parameters.name: fields

schema.description: Comma-separated list of column names to include in the export. Only applies when ``format`` is set. Unknown names are silently ignored.
schema.title: Fields

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of column names to include in the export. Only applies when ``format`` is set. Unknown names are silently ignored.

get.description:
Return aggregated project statistics.

``hours`` controls the time window for rate-based metrics
(failure rate, command counts). Accepts 1, 6, 24, 72, 168.
Defaults to 24.
Home dashboard summary - one blob the SPA renders as cards.

Recent ``task.failed`` events across every visible project.

Keyset pagination on ``(occurred_at, id)`` â€” the second element
is required to break ties when multiple failures share an exact
millisecond (replay storms, batch retries). Without it the page
boundary silently drops or duplicates rows. The cursor is
encoded as ``"<iso8601>|<uuid_hex>"`` (R4 follow-up).

schema.title: Response List Memberships Api V1 Projects  Slug  Memberships Get

get.description: List every dashboard user. Bounded by ``limit``.

schema.title: Response List Users Api V1 Users Get

delete.description:
Permanently delete a user.

FK cleanup: memberships / sessions / api_keys / preferences /
subscriptions CASCADE; audit_log.user_id and commands.issued_by
are SET NULL so historical records survive with an anonymised
actor.

Refuses to delete the acting admin (would log them out of the
operation) and the only remaining active admin (would lock the
instance out of admin UI). Callers that want a softer operation
should PATCH ``is_active=false`` instead - the Users page wires
the trash icon to this hard delete and the deactivate icon to
the soft path, so the operator picks the semantic they want.

post.description:
Admin password reset.

Sets a fresh password hash, marks ``password_changed_at``,
and revokes every active session for the target user. The
``password_changed_at`` anchor (B3) makes any session issued
before this point unusable even before the explicit revoke.

get.description:
List the project's shared channels.

All members can read - they may need to pick from this list
when subscribing to events. Only admins can write.

schema.title: Response List Channels Api V1 Projects  Slug  Notifications Channels Get
Response List Defaults Api V1 Projects  Slug  Notifications Defaults Get

get.description:
Paged admin-visible delivery log with keyset pagination.

Matches the shape of every other list endpoint â€” ``items`` +
optional ``next_cursor``. The cursor encodes the ``(sent_at, id)``
tuple to keep pages stable under concurrent insert / delete.
See docs/PRODUCTION_READINESS_2026Q2.md POL-2.

schema.title: Response List User Channels Api V1 User Channels Get

get.description:
List the caller's subscriptions, optionally filtered to one project.

When ``project_id`` is supplied the caller must currently be a
member of that project (R3 finding M10). Today the underlying
query already scopes by ``user.id`` so a non-member sees only an
empty list, but the explicit check documents intent and prevents
a regression if ``list_for_user`` is ever widened to span users.

schema.title: Response List User Subscriptions Api V1 User Subscriptions Get
Response List User Notifications Api V1 User Notifications Get

post.description: Accept a batch of signed agent->brain frames over HTTPS.

get.description: Long-poll for pending commands targeting this agent.

Render the brain's metrics in Prometheus text format.

Refreshes lazy in-memory state gauges before rendering so a
Prometheus scrape gets a fresh ``z4j_inmemory_state_items``
snapshot without forcing every subsystem to update on every
mutation (R3 finding M8).

is_outdated.title: Is Outdated
is_outdated.description: True when the agent has connected at least once and its last advertised ``protocol_version`` is older than the brain's ``CURRENT_PROTOCOL``. Never-connected agents (``last_connect_at`` is null) report ``false`` because they have not advertised a real version yet.

ApiKeyCreated.title: ApiKeyCreated
ApiKeyCreated.description:
Response from key creation - includes the plaintext token.

The token is shown exactly once. It cannot be retrieved later.

ApiKeyPublic.title: ApiKeyPublic
ApiKeyPublic.description: Public representation of an API key (no plaintext).

BulkDeleteRequest.title: BulkDeleteRequest
BulkDeleteRequest.description: Delete tasks by explicit ID list or by filter.

BulkRetryRequest.title: BulkRetryRequest
BulkRetryRequest.description:
Bulk retry request body.

``filter`` is forwarded to the agent verbatim. Common keys the
celery adapter understands: ``state``, ``queue``, ``name``,
``since``, ``until``. ``max`` is hard-capped agent-side at 10000.

CommandPullResponse.title: CommandPullResponse
CommandPullResponse.description:
Payload of ``GET /agent/commands``.

``frames`` is a list of stringified v2 ``command`` frames -
each already signed by the brain's :class:`FrameSigner` and
ready for the agent's :class:`FrameVerifier`. Empty list means
"long-poll timed out without a command"; the agent should
immediately re-poll.

ConsumerRequest.title: ConsumerRequest
ConsumerRequest.description: Add or cancel a queue consumer on a worker.

token.title: Token
token.description: Plaintext bearer token. Returned ONCE; not retrievable later.

hmac_secret.title: Hmac Secret
hmac_secret.description: Per-project signing secret (urlsafe-base64, 32 raw bytes). Returned ONCE alongside the bearer token. Deterministically derived from the brain master via HMAC, so the brain re-derives the same value on every frame and never needs to store it. Operators paste both ``token`` and ``hmac_secret`` into their agent configuration; the agent refuses to start without ``hmac_secret``.

scopes.title: Scopes
scopes.description: Catalogue of allowed actions. See z4j_brain.auth.scopes.ALL_SCOPES for valid values. An empty list mints a powerless token.

project_id.title: Project Id
project_id.description: Bind this token to a single project. When set, any request that targets a different project is rejected with 403, regardless of ``scopes``.

expires_in_days.title: Expires In Days
expires_in_days.description: Key lifetime in days. Null means never expires.

DeliveryListPublic.title: DeliveryListPublic
DeliveryListPublic.description:
Paged listing of delivery rows.

Matches the envelope shape used by the other list endpoints
(``RecentFailuresPublic``, etc.) â€” ``items`` + keyset
``next_cursor``. Cursor encoding mirrors ``home._encode_recent_failures_cursor``:
``"<iso8601>|<uuid_hex>"``.

FrameUploadBody.title: FrameUploadBody
FrameUploadBody.description:
Payload of ``POST /agent/events``.

``frames`` is a list of pre-serialised v2 frames (each is a
JSON object stringified). Sending a list rather than one frame
per request keeps round-trip count down on chatty workloads.

PoolResizeRequest.title: PoolResizeRequest
PoolResizeRequest.description: Grow or shrink a worker's process pool.

RateLimitRequest.title: RateLimitRequest
RateLimitRequest.description:
Set or clear a per-task rate limit on one (or every) worker.

``rate`` follows Celery's grammar - integer optionally suffixed
with ``/s``, ``/m``, ``/h``; ``"0"`` clears the limit. Pattern
is enforced server-side so an obvious typo is rejected before
we even mint a command row. ``worker_name`` is OPTIONAL: an
omitted / empty value broadcasts the new rate to every worker
subscribed to the broker (audit-flagged "global throttle"
path; the agent-side action logs at CRITICAL when this fires).

ScopeCatalogue.title: ScopeCatalogue
ScopeCatalogue.description: Served by ``GET /api-keys/scopes`` so the UI stays in sync.

SessionPublic.title: SessionPublic
SessionPublic.description: One active session as returned by ``GET /auth/sessions``.

StatsResponse.title: StatsResponse
StatsResponse.description: The aggregated overview the dashboard's stat cards render.

SubscriptionFilters.title: SubscriptionFilters
SubscriptionFilters.description:
Strict shape for subscription filter JSON.

HIGH-06: the service's ``_matches_filters`` expects typed fields
(e.g. ``priority`` is a list of strings). Without this model the
API accepted arbitrary shapes and silently dropped mistyped
filters, so users would see unfiltered floods of notifications.
``extra=forbid`` rejects unknown keys to surface typos early.
``task_name_pattern`` is capped to prevent pathological fnmatch
patterns from reaching the dispatcher.

TaskTreeNode.title: TaskTreeNode
TaskTreeNode.description: One node in the canvas-tree response.

TaskTreeResponse.title: TaskTreeResponse
TaskTreeResponse.description:
Response shape for ``GET /tasks/{engine}/{task_id}/tree``.

``root_task_id`` is the id of the canvas root (the original
``apply_async`` entry point). ``nodes`` is a flat list - the
dashboard reconstructs the parent â†’ child tree client-side
from the ``parent_task_id`` field, which keeps this endpoint
cheap and the rendering layout-flexible.

Standalone tasks (no canvas) return a single-node tree where
the root is the task itself.

UpdateProfileRequest.title: UpdateProfileRequest
UpdateProfileRequest.description:
Body for ``PATCH /auth/me`` - self-service profile update.

All fields optional; the endpoint only touches fields that
are explicitly provided. ``first_name`` / ``last_name`` are
structured name fields for SCIM / AD parity; ``display_name``
remains the canonical render field (derived if absent).

UserMembershipSummary.title: UserMembershipSummary
UserMembershipSummary.description:
One of the current user's memberships - the slim three-field
shape the dashboard needs for the project switcher.

Intentionally NOT called ``MembershipPublic`` - the canonical
resource with that name lives in
:mod:`z4j_brain.api.memberships` and carries the full
``(id, user_id, user_email, user_display_name, created_at,
...)`` shape. FastAPI would otherwise emit two schemas with
the same title into the OpenAPI doc and force downstream
codegen clients (ours included) onto ugly
``z4j_brain__api__...`` namespaced names.

UserPublic.title: UserPublic
UserPublic.description:
The fields the dashboard is allowed to read.

Explicit whitelist - never `from_attributes=True` directly on
the ORM model. Adding a field here is a deliberate decision a
reviewer can spot.
